oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Citron-crested Cockatoos - Fighting for Survival
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Waugh, David
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: The white cockatoo with the distinctive orange crest found on the Indonesian island of Sumba has recently been accepted as a separate species, the Citron-crested Cockatoo. Both it and the Yellow-crested Cockatoo remain classified as Critically Endangered. This article reviews threats including illegal trapping, forest loss, nest-site competition, and recent research documenting intense competition among cavity-nesting birds on Sumba. [For the full article, with images, please open the PDF link below]
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 52 No. 1 (2025): AFA Watchbird April 2025
